channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Newly discovered asteroid to pass closer to Earth than the moon Monday - NBC News
item.description:
1. Newly discovered asteroid to pass closer to Earth than the moon Monday  NBC News
  2. An asteroid discovered days ago will narrowly miss Earth  CNN
  3. Watch tonight's asteroid near-miss live online, as the enormous space rock zips past our planet  BBC Sky at Night Magazine
  4. Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online TODAY  Space
  5. Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—Relatively Speaking  WIRED

item.description:

  4. SpaceX launches Dragon cargo ship on unpiloted flight to space station  CBS News

item.title: Antarctic Sea Ice Enters 'Shock' Decline as Ocean Heat Breaks Through - ScienceAlert
item.description:

item.title: Supernova dust may be behind one of JWST's biggest puzzles - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Supernova dust may be behind one of JWST's biggest puzzles  Phys.org

  4. Astronomers Stunned by Ancient Galaxy With No Spin  SciTechDaily

item.title: The ISS travels at 17,500 miles per hour, which means astronauts inside it are aging measurably slower than people on the ground, and by the time Scott Kelly returned from his year in orbit he was 5 milliseconds younger than his identical twin brother Mark. - Space Daily

item.title: The Parker Solar Probe is now moving at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in 20 seconds, and it survives skimming the Sun's corona because a four-and-a-half-inch slab of carbon foam stays at room temperature on the - Space Daily
item.description: The Parker Solar Probe is now moving at 430,000 miles per hour, fast enough to cross the continental United States in 20 seconds, and it survives skimming the Sun's corona because a four-and-a-half-inch slab of carbon foam stays at room temperature on the  Space Daily
